zh.Woolley, Yorkshire, England, United King: 英國英格蘭越可爾伍利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Woodruff於1322年出生在英國越可爾伍利時，他的父親Oliver Woodruff32歲，母親Christian Saint Quintin27歲。他於1351年與Mabel Pudsy在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1378年之後。

zh-hans.Woolley, Yorkshire, England, United King: 英国英格兰越可爾伍利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Woodruff于1322年出生在英国越可爾伍利时，他的父亲Oliver Woodruff32岁，母亲Christian Saint Quintin27岁。他于1351年与Mabel Pudsy在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1378年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Woodruff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English woderove ‘woodruff, sweet woodruff’ (Old English wudurofe), a sweet-scented plant. The leaves of the plant have a sweet smell and the surname may also have been a nickname for one who used it as a perfume, or perhaps an ironical nickname for a malodorous person. Alternatively, perhaps a topographic name for someone who lived at or near a place where woodruff grew. There may have been some confusion with Woodrow .

History: Two English families brought the name Woodruff to the American colonies: those of Matthew Woodruff and of John and Ann Woodruffe. The latter migrated to Lynn, MA, from Kent, and moved to Southampton, Long Island, NY, before 1640. John and Ann's many descendants were established in NJ, NC, and SC by 1790. The city of Woodruff, SC, is named for this family. The name is variously spelled Woodrove, Woodroffe, Woodruffe, Woodrough, and Woodruff in colonial records.

variants.name: Woodrow
meanings: English (Norfolk): from Middle English wode ‘wood’ + reue ‘row, row of houses’ (Old English wudu + rǣw, rāw). The surname may be topographic, for some who lived at or near a row of trees, or a row of houses within a wood, or it may be habitational, from any place so named, such as Wood Row in Hatfield Broad Oak (Essex) or Woodrow in Chaddesley Corbett (Worcestershire). There may have been some confusion with Woodruff .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Woodruff was born in 1322, in Woolley, Yorkshire, England, his father, Oliver Woodruff, was 32 and his mother, Christian Saint Quintin, was 27. He married Mabel Pudsy in 1351, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died after 1378.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Woodruff est né en 1322, à Woolley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Oliver Woodruff, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Christian Saint Quintin, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Mabel Pudsy en 1351, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé après 1378.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Woodruff 1322 in Woolley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Oliver Woodruff 32 und seine Mutter Christian Saint Quintin 27. Er hat 1351 in Yorkshire, England Mabel Pudsy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb nach 1378.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Woodruff è nato il nel 1322 nel Woolley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Oliver Woodruff, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Christian Saint Quintin, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Mabel Pudsy nel 1351, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì dopo il 1378.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Woodruffが1322年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウリーで生まれた時，父Oliver Woodruffは32歳，母Christian Saint Quintinは27歳であった。1351年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでMabel Pudsyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1378年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Woodruff(이)가 1322년 영국 요르크실르 보리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Oliver Woodruff(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Christian Saint Quintin(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1351년 영국 요르크실르에서 Mabel Pudsy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1378년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Woodruff nasceu em 1322, em Woolley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Oliver Woodruff, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Christian Saint Quintin, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Mabel Pudsy em 1351, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1378.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Woodruff родился в 1322 г. в местности вулли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Oliver Woodruff было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Christian Saint Quintin, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Mabel Pudsy в 1351 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер после 1378 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Woodruff nació en 1322, en Woolley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Oliver Woodruff, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Christian Saint Quintin, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Mabel Pudsy en 1351, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió después de 1378.
